lesson_title_english: DALL-E 3 – The Prompting Secrets of the Pros
transcript_text: 5 elements of a professional image prompt: (1) Style/Medium – oil painting, watercolor, digital art, cyberpunk, etc. (2) Composition – wide shot, bird's eye view, low angle, background clarity (blur behind subject), symmetrical. (3) Scene – exact description of what you want. (4) Atmosphere – time of day (morning/night changes mood), emotional tone (calm/energetic/gloomy/bright). (5) Color palette – tell DALL-E your brand colors (black+gold, etc.). Demo: Jack Russell dog, oil painting, beach, sunny, calm, blue sea. Key insight: composition/angle change alone creates completely different images.
key_takeaway: 5-element image prompt: style + composition + scene + atmosphere + color. Changing only the angle creates a completely different image.
notes: Composition terms taught: bird's eye view, low angle, background clarity/bokeh effect, wide angle, symmetrical. Atmosphere: morning light = optimistic/hopeful.

transcript_text: Art styles demonstrated using lake image: hyper-realistic, pop-art, abstract, digital art + 3D combined, pixel style, cyberpunk. Artist styles: Van Gogh, Salvador Dali (surrealistic, floating elements, dreamlike landscapes), Picasso. Note on living artists: copyright concerns – recommends caution for massive commercial publications. Artists who lived long ago (Van Gogh, Picasso) generally safe. Style combinations possible: '3D + digital art + realistic'. Can also combine artist styles.
use_case: Art style exploration for AI-generated images
prompts_included: YES – art style prompt list provided
key_takeaway: Art styles: hyper-realistic, pop-art, abstract, pixel, cyberpunk, watercolor, oil painting, Salvador Dali, Van Gogh. Combine styles for unique results.
notes: Copyright note: living artists = risky for commercial use. Deceased artists (Van Gogh, Picasso, Dali) = generally fine. Style combinations create unique content.

transcript_text: Technique for consistent image generation: use the 'Gen-ID' from a DALL-E 3 image you like and reference it in the next generation request. This allows creating multiple images with same character/style but different scenarios. Key warning: if you like an image, ask for Gen-ID immediately before generating more – once you generate new images, the previous Gen-ID becomes harder to track. Note: not 1-to-1 identical but maintains eye color, eyebrow style, body posture similarity.
tools_mentioned: DALL-E 3 (via ChatGPT Plus), Midjourney (mentioned as comparison)
key_takeaway: Use Gen-ID from a liked image to generate similar/consistent images. Get Gen-ID immediately after liking an image – before generating new ones.
notes: Gen-ID technique = DALL-E 3's consistency mechanism. Works via ChatGPT Plus interface. Similar to Midjourney's seed feature.

transcript_text: LeiaPix turns 2D photos into 3D parallax animations. 2.5M users but free version recently reduced. Premium: 50 credits for 4K MP4, 100 credits for full package. Free accounts get 100 credits on signup. Open multiple accounts for more credits. 1,000 credits = $8. Use cases: Instagram stories, email signatures (as GIF), video backgrounds. Eden uses 3D effect in his own videos.

transcript_text: Runway ML: turn any image into a 4-second video. Access at runwayml.com. Start with Image → Motion Brush (5 brushes, each controls movement of specific image region). Motion Brush settings: forward/backward/up/down/diagonal + Ambient (noise/randomness). Example: unicorn image – brush 1: water flowing down (vertical -2), brush 2: trees moving in wind (ambient +1), brush 3: water waves, brush 4: spring movement. Generate = ~30-60 seconds. Result: 4-second animated video. Multiple similar images + Runway = connected video sequence. Note: OpenAI Sora mentioned as upcoming game-changer (not yet public at time of recording).
key_takeaway: Motion Brush lets you control movement of specific image parts. 4-second video output. Chain multiple images for longer videos. Sora mentioned as future competitor.

(4) Color adjustment: Adjust panel (brightness, contrast, shadow, highlight, saturation). Small adjustments only. (5) Retouch: face shape, V-shape, face rounding (optional personal preference). module: AI Content Masters – New Age Video
transcript_text: Manual silence cutting technique: zoom in on timeline (Ctrl + scroll) → identify silent sections (flat waveform) → split at start of silence → move playhead to end of silence → split again → delete silent section. Repeat across entire clip. Time: 5-7 minutes for typical video. Result: video is dramatically tighter, more professional. Note: AI auto-cut also available (shown in later lesson) but manual gives more control. Next step: find B-rolls.
key_takeaway: Manual silence cutting: zoom timeline → identify flat waveform = silence → split and delete. 5-7 minutes for full video. Dramatically improves pacing.
notes: Core editing skill. Flat waveform = silence = cut. Ctrl+scroll zoom is essential. This technique alone transforms video quality.

transcript_text: Two-part lesson. How to create Comment-to-DM automation. Process: (1) New Automation → select Instagram Comment on Post template. (2) Trigger: any comment OR specific keyword (Hebrew works). Set up 3 public reply variants (feels more natural, less automated). (3) Action: send private DM with message + button (link). (4) Optional: add Smart Delay before DM, add follow-up message after delay. (5) Set Live → Preview → test. Key insight: inviting comments increases Instagram algorithm reach (more comments = post shown to more people). Algorithm sees engagement → shows to more people → more organic reach. Public reply builds community feel.
cta_in_lesson: YES – promotes Brainers Club / own courses (AI Content Masters, Moneymaker)
key_takeaway: Comment-to-DM: any comment or keyword → public reply (3 variants) → private DM with link. Drives engagement AND distributes content automatically.
notes: EDEN'S SIGNATURE MECHANIC EXPLAINED IN DETAIL. Keyword triggers work in Hebrew. 3 public reply variants = feels natural. Algorithm rewards comment activity.

transcript_text: Story comment/reply automation in ManyChat. Process: Automation → Basic → Story Reply template → select specific story → set Reply trigger (any word OR specific keyword). Automatically like the reply (free). Pro: add delay before reply, auto-like all replies. Use case: post story with 'type X to get guide', keyword triggers DM with link. Algorithm insight: stories with many replies get shown to more people (Eden example: story at 200+ comments → 2,000-3,000 views instead of 1,000). Tip: don't put direct links in stories (reduces reach). Instead: encourage replies → then post link separately after engagement builds.
key_takeaway: Story reply automation: keyword triggers DM with content. Don't put links directly in stories – kills reach. Encourage replies first, post link later.
notes: STRATEGIC INSIGHT: direct link in story = reduced reach. Reply-first strategy: encourage comments → 200+ replies → 2-3x more views → then post link. Eden reveals his own story strategy.

transcript_text: WhatsApp redirect automation: when user messages Instagram with keyword → automatic reply with WhatsApp link. Setup: Automation → New Automation → 'Go Power WhatsApp' template. Trigger: message contains / equals / starts with / not contains. DM also triggers (not just story/post). WhatsApp link format: wa.me/972[phone-number-without-0]?text=[message-text]. 972 = Israel country code. Set Live. Use cases: manager who prefers WhatsApp, business with personal assistant, send to colleague, any case where WhatsApp management is easier.
key_takeaway: Instagram message with keyword → automatic WhatsApp redirect. Link format: wa.me/972[number]. Works for DMs, not just comments.
notes: WHATSAPP LINK FORMAT: wa.me/972[phone-without-0]. Eden reveals his WhatsApp strategy: Instagram → WhatsApp funnel. This is how he builds his 83K WhatsApp group.

transcript_text: Building ChatGPT-powered ManyChat automation. Setup: Settings → Fields → create two fields: 'GPT input' and 'GPT output'. New Automation → Start From Scratch → Add Trigger (Instagram comment or message). Actions: (1) Clean user input fields, (2) Capture user message → GPT input field, (3) Add ChatGPT step: System Form → Custom User Fields → GPT → Advanced Options → max tokens 200, temperature setting. (4) Output goes to GPT output field. Automation then sends GPT output as DM reply. Key: temperature = creativity level (higher = more creative). 200 tokens = very cheap per interaction.
use_case: AI-powered automated DM responses using ChatGPT
key_takeaway: ChatGPT automation: comment trigger → capture input → GPT processes → send AI response as DM. Two custom fields needed: GPT input + GPT output. Max 200 tokens per response.
notes: ADVANCED AUTOMATION: real AI-powered conversations on Instagram. Temperature = creativity control. 200 tokens/response = very low cost per interaction. Requires ManyChat Pro.
